hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (371) ran far below the Top 100 average (1,438, a 26% ratio - one of the weakest ratios of the raid), and overheal (70.8%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (66.5%) by 4.3 points - a weak 22nd-percentile kill, the raid's lowest, ranking 4th of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing despite carrying the highest item level of the five (128). Zero deaths logged.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's dominant top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136, 840 mana, 69.8% of the Top 100 sample's healing) was never cast this kill - the largest single-spell gap on this boss - with Kilsby instead running a lower Holy Light rank (guid 25292, 660 mana, 41.8% here) alongside heavy Flash of Light use (56.1%).
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Divine Favor and Mana Potion both went uncast against real 66%/79% Top 100 usage norms. No deaths to answer.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (37.6% vs 87.9% average, -50.3) ran far below the Top 100 norm - the widest gap of the raid - across 9 distinct targets; the benchmark sample tunnels healing onto a single target far more heavily on this fight than Kilsby did here.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (599) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,381, a 43% ratio), on an otherwise solid 56th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 3rd by raw healing. Zero deaths logged.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 64.3% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead leaned on a lower Holy Light rank (guid 25292, 60.2% here) and Flash of Light (35.8%).
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched or exceeded Top 100 norms - no real gaps. No deaths to answer.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (60.3% vs 84.7% average, -24.4) and top-target concentration (17.6% vs 60.9% average, -43.3) both ran well below the Top 100 norm across 16 distinct targets - a much wider spread than the benchmark's usual tank-heavy pattern on this fight.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (603) ran below the Top 100 average (1,370, a 44% ratio), but this was still a solid 58th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank - on the raid's most chaotic encounter by far, with 22 real deaths logged (nearly a full raid wipe within a kill that ultimately succeeded).
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 71.1% Top 100 share, the second-largest gap of the raid); Kilsby instead ran a lower rank (guid 25292, 40.4% here) and heavy Flash of Light (57.4%).
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Divine Favor went uncast (79% Top 100 usage); Divine Shield was cast once, self-targeted, in line with benchmark. With 22 real deaths in a five-minute span, the 'nearest' cooldown to nearly every one is just whichever cast happened to land closest in time (Holy Shock, Mana Potion, Divine Shield) - none of these read as a real, targeted save given the sheer density of deaths; this is chaos, not a specific missed opportunity.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (50.5%) and top-target concentration (13.9%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's widest spread (24 distinct targets) - consistent with the chaos of 22 real deaths.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (788) ran below the Top 100 average (1,622, a 49% ratio), on a decent 61st-percentile kill - though Kilsby ranked last (5th of 5) by raw healing despite carrying the highest item level of the five tracked healers (128), a pattern that repeats on 3 more kills this raid. 2 real deaths logged.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 77.0% Top 100 share - the single largest gap of the entire raid); Kilsby instead ran a lower rank (guid 25292, 41.0% here) and Flash of Light (59.0%).
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock was never cast this kill (28% Top 100 usage) - a real, complete non-use, not a guid-tracking artifact: Holy Shock's cast lands under guid 33072 and its heal under a different real guid, 33074, but since the cast count here is genuinely zero, there's no ambiguity to resolve either way. Neither real death (Ghostpepperx, Megatauro) lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it - the nearest casts (Mana Potion, Divine Favor) are 18-33 seconds removed.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (70.4% vs 92.5% average, -22.1) and top-target concentration (22.6% vs 70.0% average, -47.4) both ran well below the Top 100 norm across 18 distinct targets - the benchmark sample tunnels far more heavily onto a single target on this fight than Kilsby did here.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (590) ran below the Top 100 average (1,226, a 48% ratio), and overheal (54.5%) sits just 0.6 points under the sample's single worst parse (55.1%) - essentially at the bottom of the Top 100 range - on a middling 46th-percentile kill where Kilsby again ranked last (5th of 5) by raw healing despite the highest item level present. 2 real deaths logged.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 62.2% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead ran a lower rank (guid 25292, 38.8% here) and Flash of Light (56.5%).
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Divine Favor went uncast (80% Top 100 usage). Both real deaths (Lasmur, twice) land close to the kill's only Mana Potion cast (276ms-15.3s away) - a self-targeted mana cooldown, not a rescue tool, so this is coincidental timing rather than a missed save.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (50.5% vs 86.4% average, -35.9) and top-target concentration (20.8% vs 66.9% average, -46.1) both ran well below the Top 100 norm - the second-widest gap of the raid - across 23 distinct targets.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (464) ran below the Top 100 average (948, a 49% ratio), and overheal (39.6%) sits right at the sample's single worst parse (39.5%, a 0.1-point gap) - a weak 35th-percentile kill, one of the raid's three lowest, where Kilsby ranked last (5th of 5) by raw healing again despite the highest item level present. 2 real deaths logged on the raid's longest fight (482 seconds).
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: This is the one kill where Flash of Light, not Holy Light, leads the composition (69.7% vs a 54.4% Top 100 share, +15.3 points) - the benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) again went uncast here too, though by the smallest margin of the raid (-30.0 points, since the benchmark itself leans on Flash of Light more on this fight than most).
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms - Holy Shock (3 casts), Divine Favor, Divine Shield, Cleanse, and Mana Potion all landed in line. Neither real death (Ghostpepperx, Vajomee) lines up closely enough with a cooldown to call a real save - the nearest casts (Holy Shock, Divine Shield) are 4.8-5.7 seconds off but aimed elsewhere or self-targeted.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (52.5%) and top-target concentration (16.2%) both tracked in line with the Top 100 norm, across the raid's second-widest spread (23 distinct targets).

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (368) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,018, a 36% ratio - the weakest of the raid), and overheal (63.3%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse (53.8%) by 9.5 points, the largest overheal gap of the raid night - a weak 31st-percentile kill, one of the raid's three lowest. 1 real death logged.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 50.5% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead ran Flash of Light heavily (71.6%, +47.4 points over its 24.2% benchmark share) alongside a lower Holy Light rank (28.4%).
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: This is the raid's one real statistical outlier: Holy Shock, Divine Favor, and Mana Potion all went uncast against real 73%/87%/96% Top 100 usage norms - the only kill this raid where Holy Shock's absence crosses the deviation threshold (elsewhere its low usage rate keeps it from being a real outlier). Holy Shock's cast lands under guid 33072, its heal under guid 33074 - two different real guids - but a zero cast count here means genuinely zero uses either way. The one real death (Captinhelp) has no cooldown nearby worth calling a save.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (18.5% vs 37.4% average, -18.9) ran well below the Top 100 norm across 19 distinct targets.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (947, the raid's highest raw HPS) still ran below the Top 100 average (1,780, a 53% ratio), on a decent 55th-percentile kill - yet Kilsby ranked last (5th of 5) by raw healing despite the highest item level present, the fourth kill this raid with that same pattern. Zero deaths logged.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 66.3% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead ran a lower rank heavily (guid 25292, 64.5% here, +55.4 points over its 9.1% benchmark share) alongside Flash of Light (35.5%).
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock and Mana Potion both went uncast (43%/94% Top 100 usage - Mana Potion crosses the real deviation threshold, Holy Shock doesn't quite). As with Karathress and Al'ar, Holy Shock's zero cast count here is a genuine non-use, not a sign its heal (guid 33074, a different real guid from its cast) went untracked. No deaths to answer.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (46.2% vs 67.4% average, -21.2) and top-target concentration (12.7% vs 26.3% average, -13.6) both ran below the Top 100 norm across 20 distinct targets.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (496) ran far below the Top 100 average (1,558, a 32% ratio - one of the weakest of the raid), and overheal (55.6%) sits just 2.6 points under the sample's single worst parse (58.2%) - a middling 50th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank, on the raid's second-most chaotic encounter (9 real deaths within about 12 seconds of each other).
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 69.2% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead ran a lower rank heavily (guid 25292, 76.3% here, +66.9 points over its 9.4% benchmark share) - the largest positive single-spell gap of the entire raid.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: With 9 real deaths landing within about 12 seconds of each other, the kill's one Holy Shock cast (aimed at Porgrok) is the 'nearest' cooldown to all of them purely because it's the only one logged nearby - not a real save for any of the 9. This reads as a burst raid-damage event, not a case of a specific missed cooldown.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (52.1% vs 62.8% average, -10.7) ran below the Top 100 norm; top-target concentration (18.6%) tracked close to typical, across 19 distinct targets.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (489) ran below the Top 100 average (846, a 58% ratio - the closest ratio to benchmark of the raid), on the raid's best-percentile kill (64th) and longest fight (526 seconds), with 4 real deaths logged. Item level rose to 130 here, up from 128 on every earlier kill - see the raid overview for the real gear change behind it.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill either (0% vs a real 44.8% Top 100 share - the smallest such gap of the raid, since the benchmark itself leans on it least here); Kilsby's composition (Flash of Light 50.8%, Holy Light guid 25292 at 47.3%) is the closest to the benchmark's own real mix of any kill this raid.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock (3 casts), Divine Favor, Divine Shield, and Mana Potion all landed in line with Top 100 norms - no real cooldown gaps this kill. None of the 4 real deaths lines up closely enough with a cooldown to call a real save - the nearest casts (Mana Potion, Holy Shock) are 0.4-50.7 seconds removed, mostly incidental timing.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (48.9% vs 62.7% average, -13.8) and top-target concentration (14.1% vs 25.3% average, -11.2) both ran below the Top 100 norm across the raid's second-widest spread (25 distinct targets).

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: Fully consistent across all 10 kills - no slot varies at all (a real, clean result confirmed by the mechanical gear diff, not just an absence of flagged issues).
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: 8 of 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant on every kill; the Back slot (Cape, item 28765) never does - the one persistent gear gap. Consumables tell a different story: Guardian Elixir was never active on any of the 10 kills, and neither was Flask - only Battle Elixir (Healing Power) and Well Fed food were consistently up, a genuinely incomplete consumable setup for the whole raid night, not the valid Battle+Guardian Elixir pairing seen in other reports.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: All 10 real SSC/TK bosses attempted were killed this raid night. Percentiles ranged from 22nd (Hydross, the raid's lowest) to 64th (Kael'thas, the raid's best), with Hydross, Al'ar (31st), and Vashj (35th) the three lowest kills. Kilsby ranked 1st of 5 tracked healers present by item-level rank on 2 of 10 kills (48% average rank percentile) but never once ranked 1st by raw healing across all 10 - landing dead last (5th of 5) on 4 of them (Karathress, Morogrim, Vashj, Void Reaver) despite carrying the highest item level of the five tracked healers on every single kill (128, rising to 130 on Kael'thas). The most consistent real pattern in the whole report: the Top 100 benchmark's dominant top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136, 840 mana) was never cast once, on any of the 10 kills - Kilsby instead relies on a lower Holy Light rank (guid 25292, 660 mana) plus heavy Flash of Light use throughout. Target concentration ran below or in line with the Top 100 norm on every single kill, never above it - a consistently wider healing spread than the benchmark sample's often tank-heavy tunnel-healing pattern on these fights (as high as 87.9% concentration on Hydross). Zero self-deaths this entire raid. Leotheras (22 real deaths) and Solarian (9 real deaths within about 12 seconds of each other) were the two chaotic kills of the night; both still ended in a real kill. Holy Shock went completely uncast on 3 kills (Karathress, Al'ar, Void Reaver) - Al'ar is the one that crosses the real statistical-deviation threshold given its 73% Top 100 usage rate there.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: No Guardian Elixir was active on any of the 10 kills this raid night, and neither was Flask - only Battle Elixir and food were consistently up, a real incomplete consumable setup throughout. Despite carrying the highest item level of the 5 tracked healers on every single kill, Kilsby never ranked 1st by raw healing on any of the 10 kills - landing last on 4 of them.

GearCheckItems.Description: No Guardian Elixir active on any kill; no Flask either
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Only Battle Elixir (Healing Power) and Well Fed food were consistently active across all 10 kills this raid night. Guardian Elixir was never up, so this isn't the valid Battle+Guardian Elixir pairing seen as a full Flask substitute in other reports - it's a genuinely incomplete consumable setup.
